chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Cr. Rev. App. No. S – 108 of 2021 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Application in disposed of case 1. For orders on MA No.4650/2025 (U/A) 2. For orders on MA No.4651/2025 (Ex./A 3. For orders on MA No.4652/2025 (R/Surety) 04.09.2025 Syed Zaffar Ali Shah Bukhari, Advocate for applicant / surety..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- 1. Urgency is allowed. 2. Exemption is granted subject to all just legal exceptions. 3. This application has been filed by the applicant, Fida Hussain Solangi, seeking return of surety documents. As per contentions of learned Counsel for the applicant, duly supported by report of Accountant of this Court, it appears that vide order dated 23.12.2021, passed in MA No.5982/2021, filed under Section 426, Cr.P.C., the conviction and sentence of accused Sanwal, Abdul Rasheed, Ali Nawaz and Moar Ali were suspended, and they were admitted to bail upon furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- each. The present applicant stood surety by depositing four original Defence Savings Certificates totaling Rs.2,00,000/-. Subsequently, accused Sanwal was acquitted by this Court on 24.03.2025 through compromise under Section 345, Cr.P.C. Thereafter, the remaining accused, Abdul Rasheed, Ali Nawaz and Moar Ali, were also acquitted by this Court vide order dated 08.05.2025, also through compromise under Section 345. In view of the acquittal of all accused persons, the surety furnished by the applicant is no longer required. Ms. Shabana Naheed, learned Assistant Prosecutor General, who is present in Court in connection with other matters, waives notice and has no objection to the grant of this application.

Ms. Shabana Naheed, learned Assistant Prosecutor General, who is present in Court in connection with other matters, waives notice and has no objection to the grant of this application. Accordingly, the application is allowed. Office is directed to return the surety documents to the applicant after due identification and verification, in accordance with the rules. J U D G E Abdul Basit
